title: Feature: Color-Coded Multi-Member Views
body:
### Description
Assign a personal colour to each family member via their profile/settings page. Admins can also edit the colour of any other family member. Apply those colours to calendar cards, todo items, and chore cards in the Family Schedule view so each person's items are immediately identifiable. Task-specific category colours (e.g., chores, todos) take precedence over personal colours. Make colours visible across all relevant views.

This is a foundational UX feature that makes the shift from single-user to multi-person use immediately tangible—every family member's data is visually distinct at a glance.

### Why / User Value
When a family shares one calendar, it is impossible to tell at a glance whose event or chore belongs to whom. Personal colours solve this with zero cognitive effort: each card has a clear owner without needing to read names. Admins can set colours for children or guests who may not manage their own settings.

### Acceptance Criteria
- User profile/settings page includes a colour picker field (`profile_color` stored on the `users` table via migration).
- Admin settings for a family member includes the same colour picker (admin-only policy gate).
- Calendar event cards, todo cards, and chore cards in the Family Schedule view are bordered/themed with the assigned user's colour.
- Task/category-specific colours (where defined) override personal colours—documented priority order.
- Colours are visible on: Family Schedule view, Calendar month/week/day views (where item is user-specific), Todo list, Chores list.
- If a user has no colour set, a sensible default or auto-generated colour is used.
- Frontend: Vue 3 components updated; new `profileColor` prop typed in TypeScript; Inertia shared data includes `familyMembers` with `profile_color`.
- Backend: `add_profile_color_to_users_table` migration; `UserResource` includes `profile_color`; `ProfileController` handles update; policy allows admin to update other users' colour.
- Tests: Unit test for colour validation; Feature test for admin updating another member's colour; Frontend component test for colour application.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Groups & Member Roles (v1 #1), Family Invite System (v1 #2)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
AI could suggest colours for new members based on preferences or auto-assign accessible colour palettes.

### Technical Notes
- Use Vue 3 Composition API and Inertia shared props for family member colour state.
- Real-time colour updates via Laravel Reverb (broadcast `ProfileUpdated` event).
- Ensure TypeScript types for `User.profile_color` and component props.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Family Schedule View (Multi-Column)
body:
### Description
Add a dedicated "Family" view to the Calendar page that renders one column per family member side-by-side for the selected day. A fixed header card at the top of each column shows the member's name, avatar, count of timed events/todos/chores for that day, and a completion percentage. Each header card is themed with the member's personal colour (see Feature: Color-Coded Multi-Member Views). Members can be toggled on/off and reordered via drag-and-drop or a settings panel.

This view is the central multi-user dashboard for a family's daily workflow—replacing the need to switch between individual calendars.

### Why / User Value
Parents and family coordinators need one view to see the entire family's day at a glance: who has what, how far along they are, and what still needs to be done. The multi-column layout is the natural representation of "everyone's day, side by side."

### Acceptance Criteria
- A "Family" tab/button is added to the Calendar view alongside existing view options (month, week, day, etc.).
- Columns are rendered for each visible family member; column order is user-configurable and persisted (e.g., `family_member_column_order` in user settings or local storage).
- Fixed header card per column displays: avatar, name, event/todo/chore count for selected date, completion percentage.
- Header card background/accent matches the member's `profile_color`.
- Items within each column are grouped by time (timed events first, then all-day items, then todos/chores).
- Toggling a member's visibility hides/shows their column without page reload.
- Selected date navigation (prev/next day, date picker) works the same as other calendar views.
- Frontend: New `FamilyScheduleView.vue` component; `MemberColumn.vue` sub-component; TypeScript interfaces for column data shape.
- Backend: `CalendarController@familySchedule` endpoint returns events/todos/chores grouped by user for a given date, authorised to family members only.
- Tests: Feature test for `familySchedule` endpoint; frontend component test for column rendering and toggle behaviour.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Groups & Member Roles (v1 #1), Family Invite System (v1 #2)
- Feature: Color-Coded Multi-Member Views (for header card theming)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
Natural language query: "Show me tomorrow's family schedule" could deep-link to this view with the correct date pre-selected.

### Technical Notes
- Use Vue 3 Composition API; Inertia page props supply `familySchedule` data keyed by `user_id`.
- Real-time column updates via Laravel Reverb when another family member completes an item.
- Ensure TypeScript types for `FamilyScheduleColumn`, `MemberSummary`, and related props.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Assignment & Notifications for Todos/Chores
body:
### Description
Extend the existing todo and chore functionality to support explicit assignment to one or more family members via a dropdown (pre-populated with family members). Send in-app, push, and email notifications when an item is assigned, completed, or nearing its due date/reminder time. Display the assigned member's avatar/icon inline on list and calendar cards.

This closes the loop on family task delegation: assigning a chore to a child and being notified when they complete it is the core loop of household management.

### Why / User Value
Currently todos/chores are shared but not explicitly owned. Adding assignment means each family member has a clear personal to-do list, and the assigning parent/admin gets accountability via notifications—removing the need for verbal reminders.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `assigned_to_user_id` (foreign key to `users`) added to `todos` and `chores` tables via migration.
- Create/edit forms for todos and chores include an "Assign to" dropdown listing family members.
- Assignee avatar/name displayed on list rows and calendar cards.
- `TodoAssigned`, `ChoreAssigned`, `TodoCompleted`, `ChoreCompleted` notification classes created (Laravel Notifications); channels: database (in-app), mail, and a placeholder for FCM push.
- Notification bell in app header shows unread count; clicking opens notification list (in-app channel).
- Reminder notifications dispatched by a scheduled job (`SendDueItemReminders`) at the item's due time minus a configurable lead time.
- User notification preferences (email on/off, push on/off) stored in user settings.
- Frontend: Vue 3 assignee picker component; notification bell component with real-time unread count via Reverb.
- Backend: `TodoController` and `ChoreController` updated; `NotificationController` for listing/marking read; policies ensure only family members can be assigned.
- Tests: Feature tests for assignment and notification dispatch; unit tests for notification classes; frontend component test for assignee dropdown.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Groups & Member Roles (v1 #1), Family Invite System (v1 #2)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
- Natural language: "Assign taking out the bins to Alex every Monday" parsed and converted to a recurring chore assignment.
- AI reminder: Proactively notify assignee if a high-priority item is due soon and incomplete.

### Technical Notes
- Use Vue 3 Composition API; Inertia shared props provide `notifications` count for the bell.
- Real-time notification count update via Laravel Reverb (`NotificationReceived` broadcast event).
- Ensure TypeScript types for `Notification`, `Assignee`, updated `Todo` and `Chore` interfaces.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Fine-Grained Role & Permission Sets
body:
### Description
Extend the existing Spatie Permission setup to add granular, per-feature permissions for every action in the app. The current role-based permission sets (Admin, Member, Guest, Child) are fixed baselines. Admins gain a UI in each user's settings panel to toggle individual permissions on or off at the user level—overriding role defaults without changing the user's role. New users inherit baseline permissions for their role automatically via `RolePermissionSeeder`.

Example: Two users both have the `child` role, but one child is allowed to add items to shopping lists while the other is not. This is achieved by granting the specific `create-shopping-items` permission directly on that user.

### Why / User Value
Households have nuanced trust levels that don't map cleanly to a small set of roles. A teenager may be trusted to manage their own calendar events but not to delete others'. Fine-grained permissions let the admin tailor access without needing to create new roles.

### Acceptance Criteria
- All existing features have granular permissions defined (e.g., `create-todos`, `edit-todos`, `delete-todos`, `view-todos`, same pattern for chores, events, shopping, recipes, members, family settings, dashboard widgets).
- `RolePermissionSeeder` updated to seed all new permissions and assign them to roles as the fixed baseline.
- Admin UI: In each family member's settings, a "Permissions" panel lists all permissions with toggles; saving calls a backend endpoint that grants/revokes direct user-level permissions via Spatie's `givePermissionTo` / `revokePermissionTo`.
- Policies for all controllers updated to check granular permissions (`$user->can('create-todos')` etc.) rather than role checks alone.
- New user registration flow assigns baseline role permissions immediately.
- Frontend: `UserPermissionsPanel.vue` component (admin-only); permission toggle list with labels.
- Backend: `PermissionController` (admin-only policy); `RolePermissionSeeder` updated; all existing policies updated.
- Tests: Feature tests for admin granting/revoking a user-level permission; tests confirming a `child` user cannot perform an action unless the specific permission is granted; seeder test.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Groups & Member Roles (v1 #1)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
AI could suggest sensible permission sets based on the user's stated role/age when an admin onboards a new member.

### Technical Notes
- Keep Spatie Permission as the sole permission driver—do not introduce a parallel system.
- Wildcard permissions remain disabled; all permissions must be explicitly defined.
- Inertia shared props can expose `currentUserPermissions` array to the frontend for conditional rendering.
- Ensure TypeScript types for `Permission`, `UserPermissions`.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Shift Work Roster Upload & Auto-Populate
body:
### Description
Allow a user to upload their work roster as a PDF, image, or plain-text file. The backend parses the roster (using OCR for images/PDFs, or direct text parsing) to extract shift codes, dates, and times, then auto-creates calendar events assigned to the uploading user. Created events are visible in personal and family calendar views.

Shift workers often receive their rosters weeks in advance. Manually entering each shift is tedious and error-prone. Auto-populating the calendar from a roster file removes this friction entirely.

### Why / User Value
Shift workers (nurses, retail, hospitality) make up a significant proportion of households. When their roster is in Flowki's calendar automatically, the rest of the family can plan around their schedule without constant "are you working Saturday?" conversations.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Upload UI on the Calendar page (or a dedicated "Import Roster" page): accepts PDF, image (JPG/PNG), and plain-text files; max file size enforced.
- Backend service class `RosterParserService` handles: plain-text line-by-line parsing, PDF text extraction (e.g., via `smalot/pdfparser` or similar), image OCR (e.g., via Tesseract or an AI vision call via `laravel/ai`).
- Parsed shifts mapped to `CalendarEvent` records with `title` (shift code), `start_time`, `end_time`, `assigned_to_user_id`.
- Preview step shown to user before saving: list of parsed events with ability to remove individual ones.
- Duplicate detection: if an event with the same user, date, and shift code already exists, skip it.
- Uploaded files stored in Google Cloud Storage (existing setup).
- Frontend: `RosterUploadModal.vue` with drag-and-drop zone; `RosterPreview.vue` component; TypeScript types for parsed roster items.
- Backend: `RosterController` (upload, parse, preview, confirm); `RosterParserService`; policy gates upload to authenticated family members.
- Tests: Unit tests for `RosterParserService` with sample text/PDF fixtures; feature test for upload-to-event flow.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Calendar Events (v1), Family Calendar views
- Feature: Family Schedule View (Multi-Column) (for displaying imported shifts)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
- Use `laravel/ai` vision model to parse roster images where OCR quality is poor.
- AI could detect shift patterns and suggest setting up recurring events for future rosters.

### Technical Notes
- Use Vue 3 Composition API for upload state management.
- Real-time progress feedback via Laravel Reverb during parsing of large files.
- Ensure TypeScript types for `RosterItem`, `ParsedShift`.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Weather Widget for Family Location
body:
### Description
The family Admin sets a location (city/postcode) in family settings. A weather widget on the Dashboard and at the top of date-based calendar views (day view, schedule view) displays current conditions and a short-range forecast for that location, fetched from a free weather API such as OpenWeatherMap.

Weather is the most-checked daily context for planning family activities. Embedding it natively removes the need to switch apps and makes Flowki the single daily-planning hub.

### Why / User Value
Knowing the weather for the day helps families decide whether a planned outdoor activity is feasible, whether kids need a coat, or whether a barbecue should be rescheduled. Having it surfaced in context—alongside the day's events—reduces tab-switching friction.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Family settings page includes a "Location" field (city name or latitude/longitude); admin-only.
- `WeatherService` fetches current conditions + 5-day forecast from OpenWeatherMap API (configurable via `OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY` env var).
- Data cached in Laravel cache for 30 minutes to avoid rate-limit hits.
- `WeatherWidget` displayed on Dashboard (as a `DashboardWidget` entry); resizable/hideable by user.
- Weather strip at top of Calendar day view and Family Schedule view showing icon, temperature, and short description.
- Graceful degradation: if API key is missing or request fails, widget is hidden with no error shown to the user; error logged internally.
- Frontend: `WeatherWidget.vue`; `WeatherStrip.vue`; TypeScript types for weather data shape.
- Backend: `WeatherService`; `FamilySettingsController` updated for location field; `WeatherController` for serving cached data; policy: read is family-wide, write is admin-only.
- Tests: Unit test for `WeatherService` with mocked HTTP response; feature test for family location update; frontend widget component test.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Settings, Dashboard Widgets

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
AI assistant could proactively mention weather in event reminders ("Your outdoor event tomorrow—currently forecast rain, you may want to reschedule.").

### Technical Notes
- Store location in `families` table (`location_name`, `latitude`, `longitude`).
- Vue 3 Composition API composable `useWeather()` wraps API call.
- Ensure TypeScript types for `WeatherCondition`, `ForecastDay`.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Gamification for Chores & Tasks
body:
### Description
Add a gamification layer to chores and tasks: admins/parents can set weekly/monthly goals (e.g., "Complete 5 chores this week → earn a reward"). Progress is tracked and displayed in a family-level dashboard widget showing all members' progress. Completing a task or hitting a goal triggers celebratory animations (confetti, badge pop-up). The system supports virtual points and/or custom family rewards (e.g., "30 min extra screen time").

Turning household chores into a measurable, rewarding challenge dramatically increases voluntary participation—especially for children.

### Why / User Value
Kids (and adults) respond to progress, streaks, and rewards. By adding visible goals and celebrations, completing chores becomes intrinsically motivating rather than something to be nagged about. The family-level leaderboard/widget creates healthy, transparent competition and shared accountability.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Data model: `goals` table (target count, period, reward description, points value, family_id); `user_points` table (cumulative points per user per family); `badges` table (name, icon, award criteria).
- Goal creation UI (admin-only): form to define goal, period, and reward; goal displayed on dashboard.
- Progress tracking: on chore/task completion, increment `user_points` and check goal progress; if goal met, mark as achieved and trigger broadcast event.
- Family dashboard widget: shows all family members' points/progress for current period; ranked list.
- Completion animation: on task complete, trigger confetti animation (e.g., `canvas-confetti` or CSS-only); on goal achieved, show badge modal.
- Badge system: predefined badges (e.g., "5 in a Row", "Week Warrior") auto-awarded on criteria met; custom badges creatable by admin.
- Point history page per user accessible from their profile.
- Frontend: `GamificationWidget.vue`; `BadgeModal.vue`; `ConfettiOverlay.vue`; TypeScript types for `Goal`, `Badge`, `UserPoints`.
- Backend: `GoalController`, `BadgeController`, `PointService`; `GoalProgressUpdated` broadcast event; policies for goal creation (admin), point viewing (family).
- Tests: Unit tests for `PointService` (increment, goal check); feature tests for goal creation and achievement; frontend animation component test.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Chores, Todos
- Feature: Assignment & Notifications for Todos/Chores (points credited to assignee on completion)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
- AI could suggest goal targets based on past completion rates ("Your family completes ~4 chores/week; setting a goal of 5 is achievable").
- AI generates personalised encouragement messages when a goal is nearly met.

### Technical Notes
- Vue 3 Composition API; `useGamification()` composable for points/goal state.
- Real-time badge/goal updates broadcast via Laravel Reverb.
- Ensure TypeScript types for all new models.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Family Chat
body:
### Description
Add in-app messaging within the family group: a family group chat channel and direct 1:1 messaging between members. Support text messages, emoji reactions, and file/image attachments. Messages update in real-time via Laravel Reverb. Optionally, messages can be linked to a specific event, chore, or todo as contextual comments.

Family communication is currently forced out of the app (WhatsApp, iMessage). Bringing it in-app ties conversations directly to the tasks and events they relate to, keeping context together.

### Why / User Value
"Did you pick up milk?" messages currently fragment across multiple apps. In-app chat anchors family communication to the data it relates to—a message on a shopping list item, a comment on a chore—reducing context-switching and missed messages.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Data model: `chat_channels` (type: group|direct, family_id), `chat_messages` (channel_id, user_id, body, attachments JSON, linked_model_type, linked_model_id), `message_reactions` (message_id, user_id, emoji).
- Chat sidebar/panel accessible from the main navigation; shows unread badge count.
- Family group channel auto-created when a family is created.
- Direct 1:1 channel created on first message between two users.
- Emoji reaction picker on each message (at minimum a subset of common emojis).
- File/image attachment: uploaded to Google Cloud Storage; thumbnails shown inline.
- Context links: when viewing a chore, event, or todo, a "Comments" panel shows messages linked to that item; users can reply in-context.
- Real-time delivery via Laravel Reverb (`MessageSent` broadcast event on `private-family.{id}` and `private-dm.{channelId}` channels).
- Message read receipts (seen by X members).
- Frontend: `ChatSidebar.vue`, `ChatChannel.vue`, `MessageBubble.vue`, `EmojiPicker.vue`; TypeScript types for `ChatMessage`, `ChatChannel`, `Reaction`.
- Backend: `ChatController`, `ChatMessageController`; `MessageSent` broadcast event; policies enforce family membership.
- Tests: Feature tests for sending/receiving messages; broadcast event test; file attachment test; frontend component tests.

### Dependencies
- Existing: Family Groups (v1 #1), Laravel Reverb (existing real-time setup)
- Feature: Assignment & Notifications for Todos/Chores (for contextual chore comments)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
- AI can parse chat messages for action items ("Pick up milk" → offer to add to shopping list).
- AI-powered message summarisation: "Summarise today's family chat" for users who were away.

### Technical Notes
- Vue 3 Composition API; `useChat()` composable managing Reverb channel subscriptions.
- Laravel Echo (existing setup) subscribes to chat channels.
- Ensure TypeScript types for all chat primitives; strongly type Reverb channel names.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: AI/MCP Capabilities Tracker & Implementation
body:
### Description
Meta-issue to plan, track, and implement all AI/MCP integration points across the Flowki app. The app already has `laravel/ai` and `laravel/mcp` installed with a basic AI assistant. This issue identifies every user action or data-entry touchpoint that can be enhanced with AI automation, and tracks implementation status for each.

AI augmentation is a key differentiator. Rather than bolting it on later, this meta-issue ensures AI is considered as a first-class feature in every new feature built.

### Why / User Value
Family apps generate repetitive data entry (adding events, creating shopping lists from recipes, parsing rosters). AI can handle this automatically or with a single natural-language prompt—dramatically reducing friction and making Flowki feel like a smart assistant rather than a dumb form.

### Acceptance Criteria
This issue acts as the tracking hub. Child issues (linked below) cover individual implementations. The following integrations are planned:

- [ ] **Natural language task/event creation**: "Add dentist appointment for Alex on Friday at 3pm" → creates `CalendarEvent` via existing `CreateEventTool` (extend as needed).
- [ ] **Roster parsing via AI vision** (see Feature: Shift Work Roster Upload): use `laravel/ai` vision model as fallback OCR.
- [ ] **Recipe import from URL/paste**: paste a URL or recipe text → AI extracts ingredients and instructions → creates `Recipe` + `RecipeIngredient` records.
- [ ] **Smart goal suggestions for gamification** (see Feature: Gamification): AI analyses completion history and suggests realistic goal targets.
- [ ] **Chat action-item detection** (see Feature: Family Chat): AI parses messages for tasks/shopping items and offers one-tap creation.
- [ ] **AI-assisted permission recommendations** (see Feature: Fine-Grained Role & Permission Sets): AI suggests sensible permission profiles based on member age/role.
- [ ] **Proactive weather-aware reminders** (see Feature: Weather Widget): AI mentions weather impact in event reminders.
- [ ] **MCP tools expansion**: add MCP tools for all major Flowki actions (create/list chores, shopping items, family events) so external AI clients (Claude Desktop, etc.) can manage Flowki data.
- [ ] **AI assistant UI improvements**: streaming responses, tool-call progress indicators, conversation history persistence.

Each bullet should be broken out as a sub-task or linked issue as work begins.

### Dependencies
- Existing: AI Assistant (`/assistant`), `laravel/ai` package, `laravel/mcp` package
- All other feature issues (AI touches most of them)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
This IS the AI integration issue.

### Technical Notes
- All new MCP tools follow the pattern in `app/MCP/Tools/` (existing `CreateTodoTool`, `ListTodosTool`).
- Use `laravel/ai` structured output for data extraction tasks.
- Stream AI responses to the frontend via Laravel Reverb or SSE.
- Ensure TypeScript types for AI response shapes.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: Firebase Setup (Crashlytics, Analytics & FCM)
body:
### Description
Integrate Firebase into the Flowki stack to provide: Crashlytics for client-side error tracking (web and future mobile), Analytics for usage insights (page views, feature engagement), and Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) for cross-platform push notifications—particularly for assignments, chat messages, and reminders.

The existing notification system (Feature: Assignment & Notifications) has a placeholder for push. Firebase FCM is the standard cross-platform solution that bridges web, iOS, and Android.

### Why / User Value
Push notifications are the number-one driver of re-engagement. Without them, Flowki competes with native calendar/reminder apps that do send native pushes. Firebase also provides crash reporting and analytics without a self-hosted solution, dramatically reducing operational burden.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Firebase project created and credentials configured via environment variables (`FIREBASE_API_KEY`, `FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID`, `FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID`, `FIREBASE_APP_ID`, `FIREBASE_VAPID_KEY` for web push).
- `firebase-admin` PHP SDK installed for server-side FCM dispatch.
- `firebase` JS SDK initialised in `resources/js/app.ts`; service worker (`firebase-messaging-sw.js`) registered for background push handling.
- FCM token registration: on login, request notification permission; store FCM token on `users` table (`fcm_token` column); update on refresh.
- Existing notification classes (`TodoAssigned`, `ChoreAssigned`, etc.) updated to include an FCM push channel.
- Firebase Analytics: page-view tracking and key events (task completed, chore assigned, chat message sent).
- Crashlytics (web): integrate Firebase Performance + Crashlytics JS SDK; capture unhandled errors.
- `.env.example` updated with all new Firebase variables.
- Frontend: Firebase initialisation module (`resources/js/firebase.ts`); TypeScript types for Firebase config; notification permission request flow.
- Backend: `FcmTokenController` (store/refresh token); `FirebaseNotificationChannel` class; `add_fcm_token_to_users_table` migration.
- Tests: Unit test for `FirebaseNotificationChannel` with mocked FCM client; feature test for token registration endpoint.

### Dependencies
- Feature: Assignment & Notifications for Todos/Chores (main consumer of FCM push)
- Feature: Family Chat (chat message push notifications)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
Analytics data could feed AI suggestion models (e.g., "Users who complete chores in the morning have higher streaks—suggest morning scheduling").

### Technical Notes
- Use Vue 3 Composition API; `useFirebase()` composable wraps SDK initialisation.
- Firebase replaces/extends Reverb for mobile push only; Reverb remains for web real-time.
- Ensure TypeScript types for Firebase config object and FCM message payload.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.

title: Feature: iOS & Android Mobile Apps (React Native)
body:
### Description
Build native iOS and Android mobile applications using React Native (bare workflow or Expo) that provide full feature parity with the Flowki web app. Prioritise: native push notifications (via FCM), offline basics (cached data readable without network), and family data sync. Development begins after web multi-user features (v1–v3) are stable.

### Why / User Value
A significant portion of family task management happens on the go—via mobile. Native apps provide a superior notification experience (lock screen, home screen badges), faster launch times, and offline access to today's schedule even with patchy connectivity.

### Acceptance Criteria
- React Native project scaffolded (Expo recommended for faster iteration; bare workflow if native modules require it); monorepo or separate `mobile/` directory within the repository.
- Authentication: OAuth (Google, Apple) and email/password via Sanctum API tokens.
- Core screens implemented: Dashboard, Calendar (day/week/month + Family Schedule), Todos, Chores, Shopping Lists, Recipes, Notifications, Chat, Settings.
- Native push notifications via FCM (see Feature: Firebase Setup); foreground and background handling.
- Offline mode: React Query or similar caches last-fetched data; read-only when offline; queue writes and sync on reconnect.
- Family real-time sync via FCM data messages (background) and Reverb WebSocket (foreground).
- TypeScript throughout; shared type definitions reused from web where possible (extract to a `packages/types` shared package if monorepo).
- App Store + Play Store deployment pipeline (GitHub Actions `mobile-release.yml`).
- Frontend (mobile): React Native components; TypeScript; React Query for server state; React Navigation for routing.
- Backend: Existing Laravel API endpoints used; ensure all endpoints return JSON (not Inertia HTML) when `Accept: application/json` header is present; add Sanctum mobile token endpoint if not present.
- Tests: Detox or Jest + RNTL for critical flows (login, create task, chat); backend API tests for mobile-specific endpoints.

### Dependencies
- Feature: Firebase Setup (Crashlytics, Analytics & FCM)
- Feature: Assignment & Notifications for Todos/Chores
- Feature: Family Chat
- Feature: Gamification for Chores & Tasks
- All v1–v3 web features should be stable before mobile development begins.

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
- Voice input for task creation ("Hey, add milk to the shopping list") via on-device speech recognition.
- AI photo scanning for receipts → auto-add to shopping list.

### Technical Notes
- React Native chosen for code-sharing potential with the TypeScript type definitions already in the web codebase.
- Shared TypeScript interfaces extracted to a `shared/types` package.
- Laravel Wayfinder types can be used via API client helpers on mobile.
- All API routes should be versioned (`/api/v1/`) for mobile stability.

title: Feature: PWA Enhancements
body:
### Description
Make the Flowki web application fully Progressive Web App (PWA) compliant: add a `manifest.json` for home-screen installation, implement a service worker for offline caching of core assets and recent data, add a home-screen install prompt, and apply responsive mobile layout tweaks to key views. This serves as the interim mobile solution until native apps (Feature: iOS & Android Mobile Apps) are available.

### Why / User Value
Many family members will access Flowki on mobile browsers before a native app is available. PWA installation gives a near-native experience (full-screen, home icon, splash screen) with no App Store friction. Offline caching means today's schedule is readable even underground or in areas with no signal.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `public/manifest.json` created with: `name`, `short_name`, `start_url`, `display: standalone`, `theme_color`, `background_color`, icons (192x192, 512x512 PNG).
- Service worker (`public/sw.js`) registered in `app.ts`; caches: app shell (HTML, CSS, JS), recent API responses (calendar events for today/tomorrow, todos, chores) using a stale-while-revalidate strategy.
- Install prompt: detect `beforeinstallprompt` event; show a non-intrusive banner/button ("Add Flowki to Home Screen") the first time on mobile; store dismissal in localStorage.
- Offline indicator: a toast/banner displayed when the app detects no network connection.
- Responsive layout tweaks: all existing pages (Dashboard, Calendar, Todos, Chores, Shopping, Chat) verified and adjusted for 375px–430px viewport widths; no horizontal scroll on mobile.
- Lighthouse PWA score >= 90 in CI (optional: add Lighthouse CI step).
- `<meta name="theme-color">` and viewport meta tags present in the main layout blade.
- Frontend: service worker file; `useInstallPrompt()` composable; `OfflineIndicator.vue`; responsive CSS adjustments via Tailwind breakpoints.
- Backend: `manifest.json` served as a static file; `sw.js` precached asset list updated by Vite build.
- Tests: Playwright or Vitest browser tests for install prompt display; offline simulation test; responsive layout snapshot tests.

### Dependencies
- Feature: Firebase Setup (FCM requires a service worker; the Firebase service worker can be merged with the PWA service worker)

### AI/MCP Integration Potential
Service worker can cache AI assistant responses for offline reading of recent conversations.

### Technical Notes
- Use Vite PWA plugin (`vite-plugin-pwa`) to auto-generate service worker and manage precache manifest—avoids manual `sw.js` maintenance.
- Vue 3 Composition API; `useInstallPrompt()` composable for install banner logic.
- Ensure TypeScript types for service worker registration and install prompt event.
- Use Laravel Wayfinder for all route references—do not hard-code route strings.
